samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">Set to 8 to represent an 8-bit multiplier.</parameter>
        <parameter id="N_1">Set to 7 to represent the number of bits minus one for an 8-bit multiplier.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_en_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_en_in for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_en_in"/>
            <output id="mul_en_out_reg"/>
            <always>
                <description>Update the value of mul_en_out_reg on the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                <condition><input id="clk"/> has positive edge</condition>
                <condition><input id="rst_n"/> equals 1</condition>
                <condition><input id="mul_en_in"/> is 1</condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Set mul_en_out_reg to 1 when the input enable signal is active.</description>
                    <assignment_value>1</assignment_value>
                </assignment>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Otherwise, set mul_en_out_reg to 0.</description>
                    <assignment_value>0</assignment_value>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_en_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_en_in for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_en_in"/>
            <output id="mul_en_out_reg"/>
            <always>
                <description>Update the value of mul_en_out_reg on the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                <condition><input id="clk"/> has positive edge</condition>
                <condition><input id="rst_n"/> equals 1</condition>
                <condition><input id="mul_en_in"/> is 1</condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Set mul_en_out_reg to 1 when the input enable signal is active.</description>
                    <assignment_value>1</assignment_value>
                </assignment>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Otherwise, set mul_en_out_reg to 0.</description>
                    <assignment_value>0</assignment_value>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_a_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_a for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <always>
                <description>Update the value of mul_a_reg on the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                <condition><input id="clk"/> has positive edge</condition>
                <condition><input id="rst_n"/> equals 1</condition>
                <condition><input id="mul_en_in"/> is 1</condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Set mul_a_reg to mul_a when the input enable signal is active.</description>
                    <assignment_value><input id="mul_a"/></assignment_value>
                </assignment>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Otherwise, set mul_a_reg to 0.</description>
                    <assignment_value>0</assignment_value>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_b_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_b for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="mul_b_reg"/>
            <always>
                <description>Update the value of mul_b_reg on the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                <condition><input id="clk"/> has positive edge</condition>
                <condition><input id="rst_n"/> equals 1</condition>
                <condition><input id="mul_en_in"/> is 1</condition>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Set mul_b_reg to mul_b when the input enable signal is active.</description>
                    <assignment_value><input id="mul_b"/></assignment_value>
                </assignment>
                <assignment>
                    <description>Otherwise, set mul_b_reg to 0.</description>
                    <assignment_value>0</assignment_value>
                </assignment>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[0]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate partial products based on the first bit of the multiplier.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="temp[0]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <assignment>
                <description>Select the first bit of the multiplier to generate the partial product.</description>
                <assignment_value>[(<input id="mul_b_reg"/>[0]) ? <input id="mul_a_reg"/> : 8'b0]</assignment_value>
            </assignment>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[1]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate partial products based on the second bit of the multiplier.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="temp[1]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <assignment>
                <description>Select the second bit of the multiplier to generate the partial product.</description>
                <assignment_value>[(<input id="mul_b_reg"/>[1]) ? <input id="mul_a_reg"/> : 8'b0]</assignment_value>
            </assignment>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[2]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate partial products based on the third bit of the multiplier.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="temp[2]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <assignment>
                <description>Select the third bit of the multiplier to generate the partial product.</description>
                <assignment_value>[(<input id="mul_b_reg"/>[2]) ? <input id="mul_a_reg"/> : 8'b0]</assignment_value>
            </assignment>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[3]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate partial products based on the fourth bit of the multiplier.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="temp[3]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <assignment>
                <description>Select the fourth bit of the multiplier to generate the partial product.</description>
                <assignment_value>[(<input id="mul_b_reg"/>[3]) ? <input id="mul_a_reg"/> : 8'b0]</assignment_value>
            </assignment>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[4]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate partial products based on the fifth bit of the multiplier.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id="temp[4]" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <assignment>
                <description>Select the fifth bit of the multiplier to generate the partial product.</description>
                <assignment_value>[(<input id="mul_b_reg"/>[4]) ? <input id="mul_a_reg"/> : 8'b0]</assignment_value>
            </assignment>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[5]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate partial products based on the sixth bit of the multiplier.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]"/>
            <output id
